{"key_event":"1886 Constitution centralizes the Republic","reasoning":"Núñez’s Regeneración, the Banco Nacional, victory in the 1884-85 civil war, the 1886 Constitution, and Law 61 substantially increased Bogotá’s coercive, fiscal, and administrative authority, though Panama and local power still exposed limited reach. Civil society lost much of the federal, armed, secular-liberal and press autonomy it had under Rionegro; Church organization and commercial elites remained important, but often as state-aligned or regionally bounded forces rather than broad checks on executive power.","society_change":-1.1,"society_power":5.7,"state_change":1.5,"state_power":4.6}
